CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2018 Set For Trial On Jan-28-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-25-2018 Continued To Jul-25-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Off Calendar

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Apr-11-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Apr-10-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Allen J. Baden, Esq. of Edge Law Group, T:650.460.8050, for defendant/moving party;
Mary Fong, Esq. of The Mitzel Group, T:415.813.4984, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling in all respects.

DEFENDANTS WORLD TOMATO SOCIETY, INC. DBA WORLD TOMATO SOCIETY and HELEN PASTORINO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT and DEFAULT JUDGMENT is granted on the condition that defendants and/or their counsel pay $2500 to plaintiff Noise 13 Design Incorporated. The defaults entered against defendants on December 6, 2017 and the default judgment issued on February 27, 2018 are set aside. No later than April 13, 2018 defendants and/or their counsel must pay $2500 to Noise 13. No later than April 20, 2018 defendants must file a motion to compel arbitration or other response to the complaint. Defendants have shown that they are entitled to the setting aside of the default and default judgment based on surprise and excusable neglect and granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. While the conduct of defendants' counsel in not disclosing to Noise 13's counsel that the court had rejected the motion to compel arbitration papers that defendants had sought to file in August 2017 fell below what is expected of all counsel, it does not preclude the relief sought by this motion. Counsel for Noise 13 had the right to revoke the parties' stipulation that the hearing on that unfiled motion would not occur before December 15, 2017 when she learned that the motion had not been filed, but her failure to provide counsel for defendants at least a few days to file a response to the complaint before she sought entry of default also fell below what is expected of all counsel and set the stage for entry of defaults to the surprise and based on the excusable neglect of defendants. The conduct of the counsel for defendants in not filing this motion till three months after he attempted to file defendants' answer also fell below what is expected of all counsel, but does not preclude the relief sought by this motion, yet warrants making that relief conditional on payment of reasonable fees and costs incurred by Noise 13. The amount of fees and costs sought by Noise 13 is excessive and unreasonable. In the absence of any useful information about what Noise 13 actually incurred due to its seeking entry of default and default judgment, based on the court's own experience, the court has estimated those fees and costs to be $2500.

The debtor's examinations [OEX] set for April 11, 2018 are vacated.

Counsel for defendants is to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
